title: Docker Architecture, OCI Boundaries, Isolation, and Environment Evidence · Container Boundary Trace: Docker Client Daemon Api
summary: A civic data team must decide whether containers solve its reproducibility problem without claiming virtual-machine isolation or ignoring daemon authority. Planned artifact: Add and verify the docker client daemon api increment in container architecture and environment evidence map. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Trace Docker CLI, contexts, Engine API, daemon, containerd, runtime, registry, and host-kernel responsibilities for one operation.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# docker-basics cumulative container delivery portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never connect to a Docker daemon, mount a socket, or execute learner commands.
workspace: docker
version-baseline:
  engine: 29.6.1
  compose: 5.3.1
  buildx: 0.35.0
  buildkit: 0.31.1
  dockerfile-frontend: 1.25
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-environment

steps.title: Predict Docker Client Daemon Api · Theory Docker Client Daemon Api
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory team sees Docker client 29.6.1, an unexpected remote context, Engine API 1.55, containerd-backed image storage, and a process sharing the host kernel. Trace pull and run authority without calling the container a virtual machine. Competency probe: Trace Docker CLI, contexts, Engine API, daemon, containerd, runtime, registry, and host-kernel responsibilities for one operation.

content.title: Reasoning move

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example team sees Docker client 29.6.1, an unexpected remote context, Engine API 1.55, containerd-backed image storage, and a process sharing the host kernel. Trace pull and run authority without calling the container a virtual machine. Competency probe: Trace Docker CLI, contexts, Engine API, daemon, containerd, runtime, registry, and host-kernel responsibilities for one operation. A defensible solution applies Docker Client Daemon Api, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to container architecture and environment evidence map.

content.code:
# Evidence: docker-worked-docker-architecture-isolation-evidence-2
  - competency: docker-worked-docker-architecture-isolation-evidence-2
    artifact: architecture-evidence.yaml
    decision: worked docker architecture isolation evidence 2
    scenario-variant: 1
    environment: Docker Desktop VM with synchronized source files
    fault-injection: send termination during an in-flight durable operation
    retained-proof: accessible recovery runbook with rollback rehearsal timing
    case-key: worked2
    change: |
      operation: pull
      path: client -> engine-api -> daemon -> containerd -> registry
      kernel-boundary: shared-host-kernel
    verify: docker context inspect training && docker info --format json
    failure: Reject any conclusion based only on the client version or a claim that the container boots a guest kernel.
    repair: Capture client and server versions, active context, platform, runtime, storage driver, and security options separately.
content.caption: Runnable Docker artifact and transfer-evidence contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: The Docker CLI directly creates isolated processes without a daemon or API. This fails the Docker Client Daemon Api contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.
